articles.title: House tests Trump over Haiti as immigration fight shifts to the courts
articles.summary: A rare bipartisan vote in the House would force a three-year extension of protections for Haitians, even as the Trump administration and Supreme Court move to unwind the program.

articles.title: IMF Says U.S. Growth Is Holding Up, But Tariffs and Debt Pose the Bigger Risk
articles.summary: The Fund’s April outlook sees the U.S. economy expanding again in 2026, yet warns that import taxes are still lifting prices and that rising federal debt is becoming a separate stability threat.

articles.title: Scientists push CRISPR delivery closer to the clinic with cheaper nanoparticle system
articles.summary: A new gold nanoparticle platform may help overcome one of gene editing’s biggest bottlenecks: getting CRISPR safely and cheaply into hard-to-edit human stem cells.

articles.title: CinemaCon 2026 puts theatrical marketing back in the spotlight as studios race to reassure exhibitors
articles.summary: From Nolan’s IMAX showcase to Disney’s sequel-heavy 2026 slate, this year’s CinemaCon is doubling as a referendum on whether movie theaters can still command culture.

articles.title: EU copyright rules sharpen pressure on AI developers as Washington pushes back
articles.summary: A new phase of AI regulation is forming on both sides of the Atlantic, with the European Union pressing ahead on copyright and transparency requirements even as the White House asks Congress to take a lighter-touch approach.

articles.title: Nvidia’s AI juggernaut keeps growing — and so does the pressure to power it
articles.summary: The chipmaker’s boom has turned it into the nerve center of the AI economy, but record demand is colliding with supply constraints, infrastructure strain and new questions about how long the spending spree can last.

articles.title: Why Labubu still has the internet in a chokehold
articles.summary: The toothy plush monster is no longer just a toy. It has become a style accessory, a collector’s item and a test case for how fast fandom can turn into commerce.

articles.title: Philippines deepens South China Sea push as U.S. allies signal backing
articles.summary: New bases, new drills and sharper rhetoric show Manila betting that a wider coalition can deter Beijing without provoking a direct crisis.

articles.title: Coachella 2026 turns the desert into pop culture’s busiest stage yet
articles.summary: With Sabrina Carpenter, Justin Bieber and Karol G topping the bill, this year’s Coachella has become a live test of pop stardom, fan behavior and festival-era celebrity branding.

articles.title: Sudan’s war reaches a grim third anniversary as aid agencies warn of a worsening catastrophe
articles.summary: As diplomats gather in Berlin, Sudan’s conflict has become the world’s largest humanitarian crisis, with famine risks, mass displacement and a struggle for access that relief workers say is still being met too slowly.
